meaning: n.钻入水中,闪避,急速的低头
examples: The government is ducking the issue.
The kids were ducking each other in the pool.
I quickly lower myself, ducking my head to avoid looking directly into his eyes so he doesn't feel challenged.
Ducking in to El Jibarito on Calle Sol, Puerto Rican flags hung proudly from the walls.
It could end up being more dangerous to someone shooting to defend than just ducking.
